subtitle: Agentic customer readiness and execution cockpit
customer_ask: Build a secure customer readiness RAG cockpit with citations and owner handoff.
objective: Build a secure customer readiness RAG cockpit with citations and owner handoff.

blockers.message: Security readiness is 69/100. Darla needs stronger proof before this can be presented as deployment-ready.
Infrastructure readiness is 69/100. Darla needs stronger proof before this can be presented as deployment-ready.
Timeline readiness is 44/100. Darla needs stronger proof before this can be presented as deployment-ready.

snippets:
# SHI Customer Readiness Signals

SHI opportunity reviews are strongest when the team can point to a defined use case, an executive sponsor, named delivery ownership, and a measurable business KPI for the first pilot.
Customer readiness improves when the engagement includes an agreed timeline, success criteria, and a clear path from discovery to proof of concept.

# Secure Ground Truth Controls

Answers should be grounded in approved internal documents and should return citations for every material claim.
Internet access should remain disabled for sensitive customer workflows unless explicitly approved by policy.

# NVIDIA Stack Alignment

NVIDIA RAG Blueprint deployments typically align around document ingestion, retrieval, reranking, and grounded answer generation.
Agentic workflows can improve the demo story by breaking a vague request into readiness checks, evidence collection, and next-step outputs instead of returning a single chatbot answer.

next_actions: Build a two-week POC plan with milestones, blockers, and exit criteria.
Set a review checkpoint for readiness after the first ingestion run.
Complete a security review before scaling ingestion beyond the pilot corpus.
Lock approved sources and require evidence-backed answers in every demo flow.
Choose the deployment target and reserve GPU capacity for the demo environment.
Document model endpoints, vector store settings, and deployment dependencies.

thinking.ask_excerpt: Build a secure customer readiness RAG cockpit with citations and owner handoff.

steps.name: Intake Agent
steps.summary: Normalize the customer ask into a structured prompt and capture the intent signal.
steps.concluded: Objective is normalized and ready for category scoring
why: Build a secure customer readiness RAG cockpit with citations and owner handoff.
Structured ask is required before scoring and retrieval

transcript.speaker: Intake Agent
transcript.message: I converted the ask into explicit outcomes, constraints, and success criteria.

transcript.speaker: Intake Agent
transcript.message: Handing off normalized intent to Readiness Agent for scoring.

steps.name: Readiness Agent
steps.summary: Score each readiness category and identify where the project is blocked.
steps.concluded: Timeline is a priority risk at 44/100

transcript.speaker: Readiness Agent
transcript.message: Computed category-level readiness and highlighted weak signals.

transcript.speaker: Readiness Agent
transcript.message: Escalating low-confidence categories to Risk Agent.

steps.name: Risk Agent
steps.summary: Translate low scores into concrete risk language the team can act on.
steps.concluded: Security is a priority risk at 69/100

transcript.speaker: Risk Agent
transcript.message: Mapped low-readiness areas to concrete delivery and governance risks.

transcript.speaker: Risk Agent
transcript.message: Passing prioritized blocker list to Evidence Agent for source grounding.

steps.name: Evidence Agent
steps.summary: Attach supporting evidence and highlight the most relevant source snippets.
steps.concluded: Top sources have been mapped to impacted categories

transcript.speaker: Evidence Agent
transcript.message: Aligned each blocker and recommendation with top supporting sources.

transcript.speaker: Evidence Agent
transcript.message: Forwarding citation map and snippets to Output Composer.

steps.name: Output Composer
steps.summary: Assemble the final command-center view and exportable artifacts.
steps.concluded: Build a two-week POC plan with milestones, blockers, and exit criteria.
why: Top priorities are selected from weakest categories first
Exports package the same reasoning for different audiences

transcript.speaker: Output Composer
transcript.message: Merged readiness, risk, and evidence into stakeholder-friendly outputs.

transcript.speaker: Output Composer
transcript.message: Publishing dashboard sections and export artifacts.
